name: Herb or Mint Jellies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Finely chopped fresh mint (including stems and leaves), or
Sage, marjoram, tarragon, or basil (all including stems and leaves)
4 Sprigs of the herb used, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heat-resistant bowl, combine chopped fresh mint or herbs with 1 cup boiling water. Cover and let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and return to the bowl, making sure you have exactly 1 cup.
3. Add lemon juice, sugar, and vinegar to the herb infusion in a heavy saucepan.
4. Cook over high heat until the mixture reaches a boil.
5. Immediately add liquid pectin and food coloring (if desired), then continue cooking until the mixture reaches a full, rolling boil.
6. Boil for exactly 1 minute, then remove from heat and skim off any foam with a metal spoon.
7. Pour into hot sterilized jars, adding optional sprigs of herbs if desired, and seal with a vacuum lid (or refrigerate up to 6 weeks).
